ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers sunflower seeds; roasted and salted preparation is captured via classification fields.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical snack serving of roasted salted sunflower seeds is approximately 30 g (about one small handful), providing roughly 175 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Sunflower seed kernels, dry roasted, with salt added
nutrient_source.organization: Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 12038)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F09030015). Saturated fat, MUFA, PUFA, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, and minerals cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for dry-roasted sunflower seed kernels with salt (FDC ID 170562) to complete secondary nutrient fields.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for roasted salted sunflower seeds in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 35 is estimated from the closest analogous entry for sunflower seeds (raw/dry-roasted) reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per 30 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (35 × 7.22) / 100 ≈ 2.5, rounded to 3.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 655 mg/100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension suitability
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid salted sunflower seeds and choose the unsalted variety instead, as a 30 g serving of the salted product delivers approximately 197 mg sodium, which represents nearly 10% of the 2000 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~35), very low sugar (2.73 g/100 g), high dietary fibre (9 g/100 g), and high protein (19.33 g/100 g) support glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roasted salted sunflower seeds have a low glycaemic index (~35) and a glycaemic load of approximately 3 per 30 g serving, making them a suitable snack for people with type 2 diabetes when portion is limited to ~30 g to manage caloric density (582 kcal/100 g).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High PUFA (32.8 g/100 g, predominantly linoleic acid) and vitamin E (26.1 mg/100 g) are cardioprotective, but high sodium (655 mg/100 g) and caloric density are concerns
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dominant polyunsaturated fat profile and high vitamin E content of sunflower seeds support cardiovascular health; however, the high sodium content (655 mg/100 g) in the salted variety is a concern for blood pressure management, and the unsalted version is preferred for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Rich in vitamin E (26.1 mg/100 g), linoleic acid, magnesium, and selenium — all anti-inflammatory micronutrients; trans fat is 0 g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Sunflower seeds qualify for the MUFA/PUFA anti-inflammatory carve-out due to their high vitamin E and oleic acid content; limit to ~30 g daily and prefer the unsalted version to avoid sodium-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted salted sunflower seeds are the dried, hulled kernels of Helianthus annuus that have been dry-roasted to develop a nutty, slightly crunchy texture and seasoned with salt. They are a globally popular snack food consumed across Asia, Europe, and the Americas, commonly eaten out of hand, added to salads, trail mixes, or used as a topping for baked goods. Nutritionally, they are energy-dense at 582 kcal per 100 g and are an excellent source of polyunsaturated fat (32.8 g/100 g, predominantly linoleic acid), vitamin E (26.1 mg/100 g — one of the richest plant sources), selenium (79.3 mcg/100 g), folate (237 mcg DFE/100 g), phosphorus (660 mg/100 g), and plant protein (19.33 g/100 g). Dietary fibre is high at 9 g/100 g. The glycaemic index is low (~35), making them suitable for blood sugar management in type 2 diabetes when portions are controlled. The primary clinical caution is the high sodium content (655 mg/100 g) in the salted variety, which makes this product unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease who are sodium-restricted; the unsalted variant is preferred for those populations. Cholesterol is 0 mg and trans fat is 0 g. The seeds are vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal.
common_user_queries: Are sunflower seeds good for diabetics?
Can I eat sunflower seeds if I have high blood pressure?
How many calories in sunflower seeds?

confidence_reason: Primary macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F09030015, confidence 0.95). Secondary nutrient fields cross-referenced and confirmed against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170562) for dry-roasted sunflower seed kernels with salt, which is an exact food match. GI is an analogous estimate from a peer-reviewed source (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care).
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.2 g/100 g), MUFA (9.5 g/100 g), PUFA (32.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.0 g), potassium (645 mg/100 g), cholesterol (0.0 mg), and all micronutrient values not present in the HPB input were sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 170562 (dry-roasted sunflower seed kernels with salt added), which is an exact food match. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg and vitamin_d_mcg are set to 0.0 as sunflower seeds are a plant food containing no vitamin B12 and negligible vitamin D — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 3. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for roasted salted sunflower seeds exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. 4. Hypertension suitability is marked unsuitable (concern_level: high) due to sodium at 655 mg/100 g exceeding the 600 mg/100 g hard gate. 5. No -1 sentinel values remain in per_100g; all fields have been populated from approved sources. 6. This record covers the salted variant only; the unsalted variant (F09030014 or equivalent) would have a different sodium profile and hypertension suitability rating.
